commit.message:
perf/security: fix allocation, reflection, AOT, and security violations (#1040) (#1054)

* perf: replace Enum.Parse/TryParse with pre-built lookup dictionaries (#1047)

- DeltaApiHandlers: cache enum name->value lookups per CLR type using
  ConcurrentDictionary (one-time build per type, dictionary lookup per request)
- CheckoutApiHandlers: static Dictionary<string, PaymentMethod> with
  OrdinalIgnoreCase replaces Enum.TryParse<PaymentMethod>
- VectorApiHandlers: static Dictionary<string, DistanceMetric> with
  OrdinalIgnoreCase replaces Enum.TryParse<DistanceMetric>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace anonymous type JsonSerializer.Serialize with Utf8JsonWriter in API handlers

Eliminate AOT-incompatible anonymous type serialization in BasketApiHandlers,
CheckoutApiHandlers, and GraphQLHandler by writing JSON directly with
Utf8JsonWriter. This removes intermediate string allocations and ensures
all JSON output uses statically analyzable code paths.

- BasketApiHandlers: GetBasketHandler response with nested items array
- CheckoutApiHandlers: ItemsJson buffer, checkout response, confirm response
- GraphQLHandler: Introspection schema, query results, error responses;
  add WriteJsonValue helper for recursive dict/list serialization

Fixes #1043

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf/security: fix allocation, reflection, AOT, and security violations

- #1041 AOT: Cache MakeGenericType/Activator.CreateInstance in DataScaffold
- #1042 REFLECTION: Remove JsonPropertyCache reflection fallback in RouteHandlers
- #1045 ALLOC: Redirect BinaryObjectSerializer byte[] overloads to Span overloads
- #1046 REFLECTION: Replace Convert.ChangeType in ColumnQueryExecutor hot-path
- #1048 SECURITY: Reject path traversal in ProxyRouteHandler with 400
- #1050 ALLOC: Replace LINQ OrderBy with List.Sort; remove ToArray allocation
- Fix pre-existing build errors: IsEntityAccessible, LogError signature
- Update route count test assertions for GlobalSearchHandler

Closes #1041, #1042, #1045, #1046, #1048, #1050

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ci): add build_wasm job to build.yml; fix QMap include in QWT WASM patch

build.yml:
- Add build_wasm job (mirrors release.yml steps) so WASM builds on every
  push and failures surface early without blocking tag_release.
  continue-on-error: true keeps the pipeline non-blocking until stable.

release.yml:
- Add explicit '#include <qmap.h>' after '#include <qthread.h>' in
  qwt_plot_spectrogram.cpp — QMap was previously pulled in transitively
  via qfuture.h; guarding those includes broke the transitive chain and
  left QwtRasterData::ContourLines only forward-declared.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Dropbox upload, improve Rate flow, switch to Sonnet

- Fix Dropbox upload Content-Type: send raw bytes to avoid OkHttp
  appending '; charset=utf-8' which Dropbox rejects with HTTP 400
- Fix RateViewModel.onBackPressed() silently discarding upload result;
  now shows error to user on failure
- Show 'Saving...' spinner in Rate flow during background upload
- Rate batch: ask Claude for 50 titles, use all non-duplicates as queue
  instead of hardcoded 5; skip TMDB misses rather than failing the batch
- Remove hardcoded claude-haiku debug override; revert to stored modelId
  (claude-sonnet-4-5) with plain non-thinking mode

commit.message:
ux: improve new-user experience and make learning visible

Guide:
- Tiered into Quick Start (43 lines, shown first) + Full Reference
  (shown only on request). New users get oriented in 30 seconds.
- Agent instructed: show Quick Start, ask if user wants more

Greeting:
- Shortened from 22 to 15 lines
- Now surfaces automatic behaviors (freshness checks, helpers,
  warnings) so users understand the system's value
- Learning visibility: 'I learn from our conversations'
- Simpler command syntax: /guide instead of 'run command: guide'

Command system:
- Now accepts /command, 'run command: X', or natural phrasing
- All command table entries use /command format

Session cleanup:
- New Step 1: Show learning summary to user before committing
- Users see what was recorded and approve the push
- Correction capture now tells user: 'Recorded — saved for future sessions'

Feedback system:
- Clarified notes files (agent-writable) vs core docs (review-required)
- Explicit list of what CAN be edited immediately vs what cannot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): nonce-based CSP — remove unsafe-eval and unsafe-inline (C-04, C-05)

Implement per-request cryptographic nonce injection for Content Security
Policy across all 3 apps, eliminating 'unsafe-eval' and 'unsafe-inline'
from script-src.

API app:
- Remove 'unsafe-eval' from static CSP (API serves no pages)

Admin + User-Dashboard apps:
- Move CSP from static next.config.ts headers to middleware
- Generate unique 16-byte nonce per request via crypto.getRandomValues
- Set script-src with 'nonce-xxx' + 'strict-dynamic' (no unsafe-*)
- Forward nonce via x-nonce request header for Next.js SSR
- Create new response with nonce headers + copy auth cookies from
  getUser response to preserve Supabase session

Shared:
- Widen MiddlewareResponse interface to include headers + cookies.getAll
  (required for cookie forwarding in CSP response builder)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix critical and high-priority audit bugs (C-ARCH-1/2, H-ARCH-1/2/3/4/5, M-ARCH-1/2)

- RiskManagementGrain: Add ClosePositionAsync/RecordTradePnLAsync to fix unbounded _openPositions and dead _dailyPnL
- RiskManagementGrain: Update _highWaterMark dynamically in EvaluateTradeRiskAsync
- SchedulerGrain: Persist jobs via IPersistentState<SchedulerState> to survive deactivation
- SchedulerGrain: Fallback GetReminder() in UnregisterJobAsync for reactivated grains
- RealTimeDataService: Per-asset throttling via ConcurrentDictionary instead of global timestamp
- DataIngestionGrain: Add RecordTickAsync to increment _totalTicksIngested
- KubernetesJobService: Change _trackedJobs to ConcurrentDictionary for thread safety
- MarketRegimeGrain: Replace List with Queue for O(1) circular buffer operations

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
daily-build: publish release artifacts for Windows and Linux

Switch to release build and upload binaries as workflow artifacts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(script): remove shell-side upload; let terraform_data own it

The image upload belongs in image/main.tf (terraform_data.upload_image),
not in the shell wrapper. The script's role is to write tfvars and run
terraform — not to implement the upload protocol.

Remove:
- _kc_field()   — kubeconfig server/token extraction (only needed for upload)
- _do_upload()  — polling loop + curl upload (200 lines)
- Background terraform + foreground upload orchestration in run_image_module

Keep:
- Sparse file pre-flight warning (moved inline into run_image_module)
- require_cmd curl check (terraform_data still needs curl)

run_image_module now calls tf_apply/tf_destroy uniformly for all
IMAGE_SOURCE values, identical to the existing download/existing paths.
Net: -195 lines.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(dl): implement weak/strong symbol precedence in global resolver

When multiple libraries define the same symbol, strong symbols
(GLOBAL/LOCAL binding) should take precedence over weak symbols
according to ELF linking rules.

Changes:
- Split symbol resolution into two passes: first search for strong
  symbols, then accept weak symbols if no strong symbol found
- Add comprehensive documentation of precedence rules
- Add TestWeakStrongSymbolPrecedence test with both load orders
- Add test fixture libraries (libweak_provider, libstrong_provider,
  libweak_user) with corresponding source files
- Update testdata Makefile to build new test fixtures

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: 7 critical/high audit fixes in trading analysis services

CRITICAL fixes:
- HarmonicPatternService: D_XA ratio now measured as |D-A|/|A-X| (retracement
  from A), not |D-X|/|A-X|. All harmonic patterns were failing to match.
- HarmonicPatternService: Bullish/bearish direction now uses X.IsHigh instead
  of D<X price comparison, which was inverted for most pattern types.
- GannAnalysisService: GeometryConfluence now populated from OverallScore.
  Previously always 0, causing Gann to never enter ProphecyEngine scoring.
- ElliottWaveService: Added IsBullish to WaveCount and direction-aware signal
  generation. Bearish Wave 5 exhaustion now correctly signals Buy (was Sell).

HIGH fixes:
- HarmonicPatternService: Cypher pattern now uses CalculateCypherRatios with
  XC leg measurement for CD ratio (UsesXCLeg flag was declared but unused).
- MarketRegimeDetector: RSI returns 50 (not 100) when gains=0 AND losses=0
  (flat market edge case).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: document concurrent protection scope and multi-instance limitation

HybridCache prevents stampede within a single instance (one factory
call per key). For multi-pod deployments, a distributed lock (Redlock)
is required to guarantee 409 Conflict on concurrent requests.

feat: validate request fingerprint on idempotency key reuse

Compute MD5 fingerprint from ActionArguments on each request.
Store fingerprint in cache entry. On cache hit, compare fingerprints:
- Match: return cached response
- Mismatch: return HTTP 422 Unprocessable Content per IETF draft spec

refactor(upload): replace Python3 uploader with curl -F

Remove the ~100-line Python3 heredoc from _do_upload(). The upload now
uses curl natively with -F which handles multipart/form-data streaming
without loading the file into memory (no OOM risk).

Also replace the Python3 JSON action-URL extraction with grep+sed.

The only remaining python3 usage is in _kc_field() for kubeconfig YAML
parsing (with an awk fallback), unrelated to the upload path.

Add 8 new sections: Remix, Vite, Webpack, Qwik, TanStack, Docker, tRPC, Prisma

Remix (28 lessons): loaders, actions, nested routes, sessions, auth,
  optimistic UI, streaming, resource routes, progressive enhancement

Vite (18 lessons): config, plugins, CSS, HMR, library mode, SSR,
  Vitest, migration from CRA/Webpack, optimization

Webpack (18 lessons): loaders, plugins, code splitting, tree shaking,
  dev server, CSS, asset modules, Module Federation, optimization

Qwik (22 lessons): resumability, component$(), signals, useTask,
  Qwik City routing, routeLoader$/routeAction$, SSR, vs React

TanStack (24 lessons): Query (useQuery/useMutation/infinite/optimistic),
  Router (type-safe, loaders, search params), Table (sort/filter/paginate),
  Form (validation), Devtools

Docker (28 lessons): containers, images, Dockerfile, volumes, networking,
  compose, multi-stage builds, Node.js/React dockerizing, CI/CD, K8s intro,
  Swarm, security, monitoring, deploy

tRPC (18 lessons): routers, procedures, Zod validation, context,
  middleware, subscriptions, React/Next.js integration, batching

Prisma (22 lessons): schema, models, relations, migrations, CRUD,
  filtering, transactions, aggregations, middleware, testing, MongoDB

Build: 923/923 pages

chore(process): enforce TDD, CI gates, and parallel-work structure

- .github/workflows/ci.yml: GitHub Actions CI with two jobs — full
  quality gate (lint + typecheck + tests with DB) and isolated finance
  unit test job as a hard merge gate

  implementation order now starts with writing all tests first

  FIFO, position computation, TWR, XIRR, and annualized return with
  exact expected values verified against manual calculation

  called out in dependency graph; testing strategy updated to
  reference FINANCE_TEST_SPECS; quality gates section updated with
  branch protection and squash-merge configuration instructions
- AGENTS.md: workflow section updated to reference all new artifacts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: handle null ObjectResult.StatusCode as HTTP 200

When action returns Ok(data), the StatusCode is set in the constructor.
But returning ObjectResult directly without a status code yields null,
which ASP.NET Core resolves to 200 at execution time. Treat null as 200
to avoid missing cache entries for valid 2xx responses.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: close 8 self-improvement gaps in agent workflow

Session Cleanup (Gap 1):
- Added SESSION CLEANUP section to AGENT.md with commit checklist
- Agent now commits accumulated learning before session ends
- Asks user before pushing

Correction Capture (Gap 2):
- Agent immediately records user corrections to helpers/notes
- Standardized format: CORRECTION — [wrong] → [correct]

Auto-Write Notes (Gap 3):
- Agent creates/appends module_XX_notes.md during normal use
- No longer limited to formal feedback pipeline

Gap Detection (Gap 4):
- Agent flags missing helpers to user with offer to create one

Lesson Promotion (Gap 5):
- Concrete trigger: review for promotion when count ≥ 5

Trigger Keyword Fixes (Gap 6):
- Removed overly broad triggers: 'output', 'results', 'difference between'
- Synced helper headers with AGENT.md routing table

Path Fixes (Gap 7):
- Removed magpie-agent/ prefix from 12 cross-references in 3 helpers

Feedback Timing (Gap 8):
- Replaced vague 'occasionally' with 3 concrete trigger conditions

commit.message:
Move copilot instructions to AGENTS.md and update for current repo layout

- Rename .github/copilot-instructions.md to AGENTS.md at repo root
- Remove stale architecture tree entries (agents/, es-metadata.yml)
- Add examples/ and tests/ directories to architecture tree
- Remove 'Regenerating Pipeline Files' section (rebuild-pipelines.ps1 no longer exists)
- Fix Testing section to show cargo build instead of stale compile command
- Clarify agents/ is placed in consumer repositories
